message:
chore: gitignore beads br_history + multi-agent swarm scratch state

Two ephemeral patterns kept reappearing in `git status` between runs:

- `.beads/.br_history/` — the timestamped issues.jsonl snapshots that
  `br sync --flush-only` rotates on every invocation. The `.beads/*.lock`
  line above already handles the flock files, but the history directory
  itself was still tracked as untracked noise.
- `.swarm/` and `.swarm_marching_orders.md` — the orchestrator state
  directory populated by multi-agent sessions (orchestrator.md,
  marching_orders.txt, ticks.log, review_orders.txt, phase.txt) plus
  the root-level briefing file emitted by the swarm launcher. Purely
  session-local coordination; nothing that belongs in the repo.

Matches the equivalent entries already present in the aadc,
frankensqlite, and remote_compilation_helper .gitignores, so
`ru status` stays clean across the fleet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -35,6 +35,11 @@ Thumbs.db
 .beads/last-touched
 .beads/.bv.lock
 .beads/*.lock
+.beads/.br_history/
+
+# Multi-agent swarm coordination scratch state
+.swarm/
+.swarm_marching_orders.md
 
 # Coverage reports
 /coverage/
